text.title: Ollama Prompt Engineering — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-prompt-engineering methodology. Sources: Ollama documentation — modelfile / system; Anthropic prompt-engineering guide; OpenAI text-generation guide.

rule.statement: Use the exact chat template the model was trained with (Llama 3, Mistral, Qwen, etc.); never paste an OpenAI-style system prompt into a Llama-3 modelfile.
rule.rationale: Quantised models are exquisitely sensitive to formatting; a wrong template can drop benchmark scores 10–30%.

rule.statement: Encode system prompts in the modelfile (`SYSTEM "..."`) rather than re-sending them on every turn; this leverages the runtime cache.
rule.rationale: Per-turn system prompts re-pay the context cost; modelfile encoding lets Ollama cache the prefix across requests.

rule.statement: When the output must be JSON, pass `format: json` AND a JSON Schema in the prompt; do not rely on prose-only instructions.
rule.rationale: Quantised models ignore prose-only JSON instructions ~10% of the time; the `format` flag is the only reliable enforcement.

rule.statement: Use 2–4 short, diverse few-shot examples for 7B–13B models; do not paste 10+ long examples — context cost dominates quality lift past 4 examples on small models.
rule.rationale: Few-shot returns are concave in shot count for small models; more is not better past 4 examples.

rule.statement: Test the prompt against the q4_K_M, q5_K_M, and q8_0 quantisations you ship; behaviour drifts between tiers.
rule.rationale: A prompt that works on q8_0 may fail on q4_K_M; you only know by testing every tier you might serve.
